structData.title: Invalid traffic (IVT) and click fraud — detection and remediation
structData.content:
Invalid Traffic (IVT) refers to ad impressions, clicks, or conversions generated by non-human sources (bots, scrapers, click farms) or accidental clicks. High IVT wastes budget and skews performance data.

HOW TO DIAGNOSE
Call getIVTReport with your preferred vendor (IAS or DOUBLEVERIFY). Check:
  sivtRate    — Sophisticated IVT (deliberate fraud): concern threshold is >5%
  givtRate    — General IVT (bots, crawlers): concern threshold is >3%
  totalIvtRate — If above 10%, take immediate action

Call getClickLog and look for:
  suspiciousRate  — percentage of clicks flagged as suspicious
  clickPatterns   — burst patterns indicate bot activity (e.g. 800 clicks in 3 minutes at 3 AM)
  topSuspiciousIps — Tor exit nodes and known VPN ranges indicate organised fraud

Call getPlacementReport to find:
  ivtRate per domain — domains above 15% IVT should be blocked

IVT TYPES
  GIVT (General IVT): Known bots, web crawlers, data center traffic. Measured by matching IP/UA against IAB blocklists. Less sophisticated — easy to filter.
  SIVT (Sophisticated IVT): Human-operated fraud rings, hijacked browsers, domain spoofing, ad injection. Harder to detect — requires behavioural analysis.

IMMEDIATE REMEDIATION STEPS
1. Add fraudulent domains to your line item blocklist. Call getBrandSafetySettings to see current blocklist, then add high-IVT domains.
2. Block identified suspicious IP ranges at DSP level.
3. Enable pre-bid IVT filtering: IAS or DoubleVerify pre-bid segments prevent buying fraudulent impressions before they occur.
4. Remove high-IVT placements from allowlists.

PRE-BID VS POST-BID FILTERING
  Post-bid (current): You buy the impression, IVT vendor classifies it after. You still pay for the fraudulent impression but get credit/refund from the vendor.
  Pre-bid (recommended): IVT vendor scores the bid request before you bid. You skip buying the impression entirely. Reduces waste and improves actual ROAS.

CREDIT AND REFUND PROCESS
If your IVT rate exceeds thresholds agreed in your DSP contract (typically >10% SIVT), you are eligible for credit. Contact your account manager with the getIVTReport output and the date range. Credits are applied to the next invoice cycle.
